Step 9: ((🥟 ⊕ (🥟 ⊞ rot(🪵))) ⊞ 🫐) = ((🥟 ⊕ (🥟 ⊞ 🫐)) ⊞ 🫐)    [by rot]
Step 10: ((🥟 ⊕ (🥟 ⊞ 🫐)) ⊞ 🫐) = ((🥟 ⊕ inv(🥟 ⊕ 🫐)) ⊞ 🫐)    [by definition of ⊞]
Step 11: ((🥟 ⊕ inv(🥟 ⊕ 🫐)) ⊞ 🫐) = ((🥟 ⊕ inv(🪸)) ⊞ 🫐)    [by ⊕ table]

Step 13: ((🥟 ⊕ 🫐) ⊞ 🫐) = (🪸 ⊞ 🫐)    [by ⊕ table]
Step 14: (🪸 ⊞ 🫐) = inv(🪸 ⊕ 🫐)    [by definition of ⊞]
Step 15: inv(🪸 ⊕ 🫐) = inv(🪭)    [by ⊕ table]

Final Output: 🪬

To simplify the expression \( \text{inv}(((rot((inv(🐙) ⊞ (🧄 ⊕ 🐙))) ⊕ (🧄 ⊗ ((🦔 ⊗ 🪣) ⊕ (🪈 ⊗ 🪈)))) ⊞ 🪣)) \), we follow these steps:

1. Apply the inverse transformation to \( 🐙 \):
   \[
   \text{inv}(🐙) = 🪈
   \]
   So, the expression becomes:
   \[
   \text{inv}(((rot((🪈 ⊞ (🧄 ⊕ 🐙))) ⊕ (🧄 ⊗ ((🦔 ⊗ 🪣) ⊕ (🪈 ⊗ 🪈)))) ⊞ 🪣))
   \]

2. Simplify \( (🧄 ⊕ 🐙) \):
   \[
   🧄 ⊕ 🐙 = 🦔
   \]
   So, the expression becomes:
   \[
   \text{inv}(((rot((🪈 ⊞ 🦔))) ⊕ (🧄 ⊗ ((🦔 ⊗ 🪣) ⊕ (🪈 ⊗ 🪈)))) ⊞ 🪣)
   \]

3. Apply the definition of \( ⊞ \):
   \[
   🪈 ⊞ 🦔 = (🪈 ⊗ 🦔) ⊗ 🪈
   \]
   First, compute \( (🪈 ⊗ 🦔) \):
   \[
   🪈 ⊗ 🦔 = 🦔
   \]
   Then, compute \( (🦔 ⊗ 🪈) \):
   \[
   🦔 ⊗ 🪈 = 🦔
   \]
   So, the expression becomes:
   \[
   \text{inv}(((rot(🦔)) ⊕ (🧄 ⊗ ((🦔 ⊗ 🪣) ⊕ (🪈 ⊗ 🪈)))) ⊞ 🪣)
   \]

4. Apply the rotation transformation to \( 🦔 \):
   \[
   \text{rot}(🦔) = 🪣
   \]
   So, the expression becomes:
   \[
   \text{inv}((🪣 ⊕ (🧄 ⊗ ((🦔 ⊗ 🪣) ⊕ (🪈 ⊗ 🪈)))) ⊞ 🪣)
   \]

5. Simplify \( (🦔 ⊗ 🪣) \):
   \[
   🦔 ⊗ 🪣 = 🪈
   \]
   So, the expression becomes:
   \[
   \text{inv}((🪣 ⊕ (🧄 ⊗ (🪈 ⊕ (🪈 ⊗ 🪈)))) ⊞ 🪣)
   \]

6. Simplify \( (🪈 ⊗ 🪈) \):
   \[
   🪈 ⊗ 🪈 = 🪣
   \]
   So, the expression becomes:
   \[
   \text{inv}((🪣 ⊕ (🧄 ⊗ (🪈 ⊕ 🪣))) ⊞ 🪣)
   \]

7. Simplify \( (🪈 ⊕ 🪣) \):
   \[
   🪈 ⊕ 🪣 = 🪈
   \]
   So, the expression becomes:
   \[
   \text{inv}((🪣 ⊕ (🧄 ⊗ 🪈)) ⊞ 🪣)
   \]

8. Simplify \( (🧄 ⊗ 🪈) \):
   \[
   🧄 ⊗ 🪈 = 🪬
   \]
   So, the expression becomes:
   \[
   \text{inv}((🪣 ⊕ 🪬) ⊞ 🪣)
   \]

9. Simplify \( (🪣 ⊕ 🪬) \):
   \[
   🪣 ⊕ 🪬 = 🦔
   \]
   So, the expression becomes:
   \[
   \text{inv}(🦔 ⊞ 🪣)
   \]

10. Apply the definition of \( ⊞ \):
    \[
    🦔 ⊞ 🪣 = (🦔 ⊗ 🪣) ⊗ 🦔
    \]
    First, compute \( (🦔 ⊗ 🪣) \):
    \[
    🦔 ⊗ 🪣 = 🪈
    \]
    Then, compute \( (🪈 ⊗ 🦔) \):
    \[
    🪈 ⊗ 🦔 = 🦔
    \]
    So, the expression becomes:
    \[
    \text{inv}(🦔)
    \]

11. Apply the inverse transformation to \( 🦔 \):
    \[
    \text{inv}(🦔) = 🧄
    \]

Thus, the final simplified expression is:

$\boxed{🧄}$

Step 9: (rot((🥟 ⊕ 🪬)) ⊞ inv(rot((🥟 ⊗ 🪭)))) = (rot(🥟) ⊞ inv(rot((🥟 ⊗ 🪭))))    [by ⊕ table]
Step 10: (rot(🥟) ⊞ inv(rot((🥟 ⊗ 🪭)))) = (🪭 ⊞ inv(rot((🥟 ⊗ 🪭))))    [by rot]
Step 11: (🪭 ⊞ inv(rot((🥟 ⊗ 🪭)))) = (🪭 ⊞ inv(rot(🥟)))    [by ⊗ table]
Step 12: (🪭 ⊞ inv(rot(🥟))) = (🪭 ⊞ inv(🪭))    [by rot]
